title: Bubble and squeak
subtitle: Leftover potatoes and cabbage fried into a crisp cake
excerpt: Mashed potato and cooked cabbage or Brussels sprouts combined, shaped into a cake, and fried in butter until crusted on both sides. The classic British use for leftover roast vegetables. The outsides must form a proper golden crust — the contrast with the soft interior is the whole point.
sourceNotes: Bubble and squeak is one of the oldest documented British leftover dishes, appearing in cookbooks from the 18th century originally as a fried beef and vegetable hash. By the 19th century it had settled into the potato-and-greens form that is now standard. The name comes from the sounds the vegetables make in the frying pan.

content.text: The ratio is roughly two parts potato to one part greens. Cooked roast potatoes, mashed potatoes, or leftover boiled potatoes all work. The greens should be cooked and well-drained — wet greens will make the cake fall apart in the pan.
Resist the urge to move it in the pan. The crust forms by leaving it undisturbed on each side for 5 to 7 minutes. If you keep stirring it, you get soft scrambled potato rather than a crispy cake.

Combine the {{potato}} and {{cabbage-savoy}} in a bowl. Season well with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Form into a rough cake shape. Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a heavy frying pan over medium-high heat until foaming. Add the potato cake and press it down lightly with a spatula. Cook undisturbed for 6 to 7 minutes, until the base is deeply golden and crusted. Carefully flip using a large spatula (or invert onto a plate and slide back in). Cook the second side for a further 5 to 6 minutes until equally golden. Serve immediately from the pan, cut into wedges.

items.cause: Too much moisture in the greens, or mixture too loose
items.fix: Squeeze cooked greens hard before adding to the potato. If the mixture is very wet, add a tablespoon of plain flour to bind

items.symptom: Pale and soft on the outside rather than crusted
items.cause: Heat too low, or butter not hot enough before adding the cake
items.fix: The butter must be foaming before the cake goes in. Medium-high heat is needed to develop a crust; medium will just slowly heat the cake through

content.text: Bubble and squeak is a Boxing Day and post-Sunday-roast dish, made from whatever roast vegetables are left over. It is eaten as a side with cold meats and pickles, or as a main topped with a fried egg. The name is more evocative than the dish is complex, which is perhaps why it has stayed in the language.
